---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
# Load and plot
library(quantmod)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggthemes)
USO <- getSymbols("USO",env=NULL ) %>% as.data.frame() 
VIX <- getSymbols("^VIX",env=NULL ) %>% as.data.frame() 
USO <- USO %>% rownames_to_column("date") %>% dplyr::select(date, USO.Adjusted)
VIX <- VIX %>% rownames_to_column("date") %>% dplyr::select(date, VIX.Adjusted)
df <- Reduce(function(...) full_join(..., by = "date"), list(USO, VIX)) %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = -date, names_to = "ticker") %>% 
    mutate(ticker=sub(".Adjusted", "", ticker), date=as.Date(date)) %>% arrange(date)
df %>% ggplot(aes(x=date,y=value,color=ticker,group=ticker)) + geom_line() + scale_color_colorblind()
```
```{r}

df <- df %>% mutate(wday = wday(date, label = TRUE)) %>% group_by(ticker) %>% 
    mutate(ret = c(0, diff(log(value)))) %>% ungroup
df <- df %>% group_by(ticker) %>% mutate(up = lag(ret) > 0) %>% ungroup
df_uso <- df %>% dplyr::filter(ticker == "USO") %>% dplyr::select(date, up) %>% rename(USOup=up)
df_vix <- df %>% dplyr::filter(ticker == "VIX") %>% dplyr::select(date, value)  %>% rename(VIX=value)
df_full <- full_join(df, df_vix, by="date") %>% full_join(., df_uso, by="date") %>% mutate(date=as.Date(date)) 
```

```{r}

df_full %>% dplyr::filter(ticker == "USO") %>% group_by(wday) %>% na.omit %>% 
    reframe(M = mean(ret*100, na.rm=T), S = sd(ret*100, na.rm=T)/sqrt(n())  )  %>% 
    ggplot(aes(x=wday, y=M, ymin=M-S, ymax=M+S)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", position="dodge", width = 0.5) + geom_errorbar(width=0.25, position = position_dodge(0.5))
df_full %>% dplyr::filter(ticker == "USO") %>% group_by(wday, USOup) %>% na.omit %>% 
    reframe(M = mean(ret*100, na.rm=T), S = sd(ret*100, na.rm=T)/sqrt(n())  )  %>% 
    ggplot(aes(x=wday, y=M, ymin=M-S, ymax=M+S, fill=USOup)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", position="dodge", width = 0.5) + geom_errorbar(width=0.25, position = position_dodge(0.5))

```
```{r}
df_full %>% mutate(D = runPercentRank(VIX %>% na.locf(na.rm=F), 252) %>% ntile(5)) %>% dplyr::filter(ticker == "USO") %>% group_by(D,wday) %>% na.omit %>% 
    reframe(M = mean(ret*100, na.rm=T), S = sd(ret*100, na.rm=T)/sqrt(n())  )  %>% 
    ggplot(aes(x=D, y=M, ymin=M-S, ymax=M+S)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", position="dodge", width = 0.5) + geom_errorbar(width=0.25, position = position_dodge(0.5)) + facet_wrap(~wday)

```


```{r}

df_bt <- df_full %>% mutate(D = runPercentRank(VIX %>% na.locf(na.rm=F), 252) %>% ntile(5))%>% na.omit %>% dplyr::filter(wday=="lun" & ticker == "USO") %>% mutate(naive_pnl = ret * -1, adv_pnl = -ret*(D==5)) %>% na.omit %>% mutate(naive_eq = cumsum(naive_pnl), adv_eq = cumsum(adv_pnl)) 
df_bt %>% ggplot(aes(date)) + geom_line(aes(y=naive_eq), color="black") + geom_line(aes(y=adv_eq), color="blue")
mean(df_bt$naive_pnl) / sd(df_bt$naive_pnl) * sqrt(52)
mean(df_bt$adv_pnl) / sd(df_bt$adv_pnl) * sqrt(52)
```

```{r}

df_bt <- df_full %>% mutate(D = runPercentRank(VIX %>% na.locf(na.rm=F), 252) %>% ntile(5))%>% na.omit %>% dplyr::filter(ticker == "USO") %>% mutate(naive_pnl = ret * -1, adv_pnl = -ret*(D==5)) %>% na.omit %>% mutate(naive_eq = cumsum(naive_pnl), adv_eq = cumsum(adv_pnl)) 
df_bt %>% ggplot(aes(date)) + geom_line(aes(y=naive_eq), color="black") + geom_line(aes(y=adv_eq), color="blue")
mean(df_bt$naive_pnl) / sd(df_bt$naive_pnl) * sqrt(52)
mean(df_bt$adv_pnl) / sd(df_bt$adv_pnl) * sqrt(52)
```

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
